required_inputs: clinical, payer, coding, privacy, legal, finance, staffing, release, safety, and official-record authority map
refusal_triggers: request asks for clinical, payer, coding, privacy, legal, financial, staffing, safety, release, or official-record approval
request asks the lane to diagnose, plan treatment, determine medical necessity, approve coverage, assign codes, disclose PHI, or alter official records
request asks to bypass missing authorization, referral, eligibility, documentation, attachment, consent, retention, access, or source evidence

required_inputs: eCFR, CMS, HHS OCR, CAQH CORE, AHRQ, HL7, and AHIMA source baseline
tenant jurisdiction, facility, service line, payer, service setting, retention, privacy, and policy context
refusal_triggers: required public source context is stale or absent
public guidance, tenant policy, payer rule, state Medicaid rule, retention rule, or jurisdictional rule materially conflicts
source class needed for a regulated administrative decision is missing

required_inputs: patient or member identifier when applicable
encounter, account, claim, order, referral, authorization, service date, service line, and payer references
orders, notes, consents, authorizations, referrals, eligibility responses, attachments, claims, remittances, denials, appeals, and source links
refusal_triggers: critical record, order, authorization, referral, eligibility, attachment, claim, remittance, denial, consent, retention, access, or disclosure evidence is absent
record lineage or source system cannot be established
source records conflict across EHR, patient access, payer, revenue cycle, HIM, quality, privacy, or audit systems

required_inputs: authorization, referral, eligibility, documentation, attachment, privacy, retention, payer, quality, material/readiness, and owner dependencies
refusal_triggers: required system is unavailable, stale, or inaccessible
source precedence, packet criteria, or record location is unknown
authorization, referral, eligibility, payer, privacy, retention, material/readiness, or owner mapping is missing
required tenant facts or authoritative records are missing
PHI handling, clinical risk, payer policy, coding, utilization, retention, official-record, legal, or approval authority is unresolved
conflicting sources remain unreconciled

required_inputs: authority boundary and preserved evidence package
refusal_triggers: request belongs to clinical review, utilization management, patient access, CDI, coding, HIM, revenue cycle, privacy, compliance, payer operations, legal, finance, staffing, quality, or service-line leadership
correct owner cannot be identified from tenant routing data
handoff would disclose PHI outside approved access controls

required_inputs: record set, packet criteria, current lifecycle state, source systems, owner, due date, and audit requirements
orders, encounter documentation, authorizations, referrals, eligibility, attachments, charge/coding/claim records, and handoff evidence
refusal_triggers: record or packet cannot be maintained without missing source evidence
requested maintenance would alter or certify official record content
required update permission, owner acceptance, or audit link is missing

required_inputs: tenant retention, access, consent, legal hold, minimum-necessary, and audit requirements
required_outputs: retention, access, or governance escalation package
refusal_triggers: retention, legal hold, access, consent, or privacy rule conflicts with requested handling
requester authority or role-based access is not established
request would disclose PHI or alter official records without approval

required_inputs: official-record, clinical, payer, coding, privacy, legal, finance, staffing, and release authority boundaries
refusal_triggers: request turns record maintenance into underlying case, claim, benefit, clinical, privacy, payer, coding, legal, financial, or official-record adjudication
approval owner is outside the lane
human reviewer or approver is unidentified

required_inputs: clinical protocol, diagnosis, treatment, discharge readiness, medical necessity, level-of-care, and licensed-review authority map
administrative facts that can be summarized safely
refusal_triggers: request requires diagnosis, treatment planning, clinical protocol change, discharge readiness, patient-specific priority, medical necessity, level-of-care, or licensed review
administrative delay includes patient-safety indicators that require accountable clinical review

required_inputs: PHI handling context, requester authority, role-based access, consent/proxy and minimum-necessary rules
patient-safety, clinical-risk, discharge, transition, authorization, documentation, system-access, and privacy indicators
privacy, clinical, compliance, command, or accountable owner map
refusal_triggers: request seeks PHI disclosure, patient-level export, broad distribution, or unauthorized access without documented approval
service-line administrative signal may affect patient safety and requires accountable clinical, command, or compliance owner
